authority: Official Pyzel model descriptions and governed Quivrr catalogue records

models.reason: A modernised 1970s keel-fish concept built around speed, volume, glide and small-wave fun.
Fish-derived speed and planing remain central, with a more pulled-in outline and added manoeuvrability.
The XL variant prioritises easy paddling, extra support and weak-wave speed over the base model's fish identity.
A twin-fin evolution of the Mid Length Crisis, with glide, flow and longer-rail performance defining its role.
The Ghost is a quality-wave performance shortboard designed for powerful surf, hold, sensitivity and technical surfing.
The pro file is an even more refined competition-oriented Ghost variant for demanding good-wave performance.
Tail variation does not change the Ghost's defining high-performance identity in quality and powerful surf.
Extra foam and support do not change the base Ghost's performance-shortboard design role.
Weak-wave speed, easy paddling and compact funformance are the Gremlin's defining intent.
The XL version retains the Gremlin's weak-wave role while adding support, paddle power and volume.
Flat rocker, full outline, easy paddling and weak-wave speed make small-wave performance the primary role.
Designed to maximise technical performance in average-to-good surf with high-performance rocker and response.
Explicit mid-length design combining paddle, glide and looser responsive surfing.
A shorter, fuller and more stable Ghost-derived board for smaller everyday surf.
The compact Ghost platform adds speed, paddle and stability for everyday waves while retaining a performance edge.
Explicit step-up design with added paddle, rocker and hold for solid reef, beach and travel surf.
Designed around early entry, control and manoeuvrability in large and powerful waves.
Pyzel explicitly positions the Phantom as the everyday step-down counterpart to the Ghost.
Round-tail variant retains the Phantom's everyday performance role.
Squash-tail variant retains the Phantom's everyday performance role.
XL dimensions add support without changing the Phantom's daily-driver identity.
Built to bridge Red Tiger and Ghost for bigger, better and more powerful surf, with performance remaining primary.
The grom version retains the Power Tiger's performance role rather than becoming a step-up by size label alone.
The XL version retains performance-shortboard identity with additional support for larger surfers.
Explicit one-board-quiver intent with easy paddling, broad wave range and a snappy performance feel.
Purpose-built for late drops and heavy barrels at Puerto Escondido, based on the Padillac platform.
A shorter, wider all-rounder with easy paddle and high-performance response.
Explicit one-board-quiver evolution with broader performance and weak-wave range.
The XL model retains the Pyzalien 2's daily-driver role while increasing support and volume.
A technical performance model designed around speed, precision and high-level surfing.
John John Florence's HPSB for medium, slower and weaker surf, retaining high-performance intent despite its small-wave range.
The XL version retains Red Tiger performance identity with increased support and volume.
Explicitly described as a rippable daily driver that maximises wave count without losing sensitivity and performance.
Refined high-performance design derived from Phantom DNA for technical competition-style surfing.
The XL version retains the Shadow's high-performance identity with additional support.
Designed for major North Shore surf with maximum paddle, drive, control and manoeuvrability in big waves.
A blend of Happy Twin and White Tiger for easy speed, weaker surf and responsive small-wave performance.
Stubby average-to-below-average-wave design built to create speed through weak sections while retaining progressive performance.
A timeless channel-bottom twin combining easy acceleration, broad wave range and modern performance rather than fish-first intent.

review_notes: All 40 governed Pyzel models have an explicit public family and detailed category.
Ghost and all Ghost performance variants are governed as Performance Shortboards; no Ghost model is treated as a generic Daily Driver.
Phantom remains the primary performance Daily Driver in the Ghost family.
Power Tiger and its variants are Performance Shortboards, not Step Ups, because their core purpose is high-performance surfing in better and more powerful waves.
Red Tiger remains a small-wave High Performance Shortboard rather than a Groveller.
White Tiger, Tiger Twin, Gremlin and Happy Twin occupy the small-wave Groveller lane.
Construction and XL variants inherit the underlying model family unless manufacturer intent materially changes the design role.
